Recursion

Recursion is a programming technique where a function calls itself as a subroutine. This allows the function to be repeated several times from different parts of its code.

Let's consider an example: calculating factorial (denoted by `n!`) using recursion:

**Example:** Calculate `5!` (`5 * 4 * 3 * 2 * 1`)

```python
def recursive_factorial(n):
    if n == 0 or n == 1:
        return 1
    else:
        return n * recursive_factorial(n-1)
```

Abstract
NESTIQ is a web-based real estate portal built using Java EE, Servlets, and JSP . The platform
connects Buyers looking for properties, Sellers listing their real estate, and Administrators
who oversee platform safety and moderation. The project follows a Model-View-Controller
(MVC) architecture without relying on external frameworks, ensuring that fundamental
Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) concepts are manually implemented and clearly vis-
ible in the codebase.
The system supports three user roles. Buyers can search for properties, add them to
carts/wishlists, book viewings, and leave reviews. Sellers can register , list properties, man-
age their stock, and confirm bookings. Admins approve new users, moderate reviews, and
oversee platform activity.
Beyond the six core CRUD modules—Admin, Property, Booking, Seller , Review, and Cart/Wish-
list—the application includes several advanced features. A custom Binary Search Tree
(BST) and QuickSort algorithm power the property search and filtering features. A flat-
file persistence layer handles all database operations purely through Java File I/O. The
codebase was organized so each team member could work independently on their CRUD
without stepping on each other’s changes.
This report documents the system architecture, walks through each CRUD module, lists
the special functionalities, shows the structural relationships, and records the work each
team member contributed.

1. Introduction
1.1 Project Background
Finding a verified property to buy or rent traditionally involved navigating fragmented
listings, unverified sellers, and disorganized booking processes. Buyers rarely had a clean
way to track viewings, while Sellers struggled to manage inquiries and property stock si-
multaneously.
NESTIQ is our team’s answer to that problem. It is a single web platform where Buyers can
search for properties, see verified seller details and ratings, book a viewing, and leave a
review afterward. Sellers get a clean dashboard to manage their listings, confirm viewings,
and track properties. Admins keep the platform safe by managing users and moderating
feedback.
1.2 Project Objectives
- Build a working real estate platform that handles the full journey from signup, to
property search, to booking, to review.
- Apply object-oriented programming concepts cleanly: encapsulation, inheritance,
abstraction, and polymorphism.
- Give each of the six team members a clear area of ownership so the work could
happen in parallel.
- Implement custom Data Structures and Algorithms (BST, QuickSort) for fast in-memory
searching and sorting.
- Utilize flat-file data persistence (TXT/TSV) to demonstrate pure Java file handling.
1.3 Scope
The platform supports three user roles and six core modules: Admin, Property, Booking,
Seller , Review, and Cart/Wishlist. It runs as a web application, with the front end rendered
using JSP/JSTL templates and the back end powered by Java Servlets. Persistence is han-
dled via Flat-files.
1.4 Technology Stack
Layer Technologies Used
Language Java 8 (javac 1.8)
Framework Java Servlets 4.0.1, JSP / JSTL 1.2
Build Tool Apache Maven 3.x
Server Apache Tomcat 8.x
View Engine JSP templates with HTML5 / CSS3 / Vanilla JS
Database Flat-files (.txt, .tsv) in WEB-INF
Version Control Git + GitHub (branch-per-CRUD workflow)
3

2. System Overview & Architecture
2.1 The MVC Layer Pattern
Every module in NESTIQ is built using the Model-View-Controller pattern. This makes the
codebase predictable: once you understand how one CRUD works, the other five make
immediate sense.
- Model: Plain Java classes that map to real-world entities (e.g.,User, Property, Reservation).
They encapsulate data and basic validation.
- Service / Persistence: Classes that encapsulate business logic and flat-file I/O. They
read/write from .txt files using BufferedReader/PrintWriter.
- Controller: Servlet classes extending HttpServlet. They handle GET/POST requests,
call the services, and forward to JSP views.
- View: .jsp files that render the HTML to the user .
2.2 Why This Pattern Matters
Splitting the app into layers gives us real benefits:
- Easier to share: Six team members worked on six CRUDs in parallel. Everyone
touched their own servlet and model files.
- Easier to read: If something is wrong with file saving, you look in the Service. If a
URL is broken, you look in the Servlet.
- Easier to extend: Adding a new feature means adding methods to the right layer
without rewriting the whole application.
2.3 OOP Concepts at Work
OOP Concept Where It Appears in NESTIQ
Encapsulation Every Model has private fields and public getters/setters. Out-
side code goes through methods.
Inheritance PublicReview and VerifiedReview extend Review. All Con-
trollers extend HttpServlet.
Polymorphism Method overriding used in displayReview() across the Review
hierarchy. Servlets override doGet and doPost.
Abstraction HttpServlet hides HTTP networking complexities. File I/O com-
plexities are abstracted behind Service methods.
Composition InquiryThread has-a List<InquiryMessage>. Inner classes are
used for the PropertyBST.
2.4 SOLID Principles Applied
- Single Responsibility Principle: Models hold data. Servlets process HTTP .LoginService
only handles authentication.
4

- Open/Closed Principle: Servlets can be extended for new features without changing
the abstract base HttpServlet.
- Liskov Substitution Principle: VerifiedReview can be used anywhere a Review is
expected without breaking view logic.
5

3. Class Diagrams & Relationships
3.1 Entity Relationships
The system is built on 9 core domain entities. Their relationships dictate the flow of data:
- Admin: Manages all CRUDs implicitly without strict database foreign keys. Respon-
sible for access control and moderation.
- User → Property (Seller): One Seller can list many Properties (1-to-many).
- User → Reservation (Buyer): One Buyer can have many Bookings (1-to-many).
- Property → Reservation: One Property can have many Bookings over time (1-to-
many).
- User → Review: A Buyer can leave many reviews (1-to-many).
- Property → Review: A Property can receive many reviews (1-to-many).
3.2 MVC Layered Architecture
A standard HTTP request follows a strict horizontal path:
1. Request hits /search → SearchServlet.doGet()
2. SearchServlet instantiates needed Services/Models.
3. Flat-file data is read and mapped to Property objects.
4. Data structure (e.g., PropertyBST) filters the list.
5. RequestDispatcher forwards the resulting list to search.jsp.
6

4. CRUD Modules
4.1 Admin Management CRUD
Module Owner De Silva K. H. B. N. - IT25103795
Primary Files AdminDashboardServlet, DeleteUserServlet, ChangeRoleServlet,
AdminAnnouncementServlet, AdminDeleteReviewServlet
Overview: The Admin CRUD allows platform administrators to oversee the entire system.
Admins manage user roles, delete policy-violating accounts, moderate inappropriate re-
views, and broadcast platform-wide announcements.
Key Functionalities: Role management, User deletion, System analytics viewing, and Re-
view moderation.
OOP Concepts: Abstraction (hiding moderation logic inside admin services), Encapsulation
(protecting user roles from unauthorized edits).
4.2 Property (Product) Management CRUD
Module Owner SAMARAKOON S.M.D.S. - IT25103995
Primary Files PropertyServlet, AddPropertyServlet, UpdatePropertyServlet,
DeletePropertyServlet, Property.java
Overview: Handles the core entity of the platform. Sellers can create, read, update, and
delete real estate listings.
Key Functionalities: Full text search, listing creation with image URLs, status updates,
and dynamic listing display.
4.3 Book & Returning Management CRUD
Module Owner Wijesuriya W.A.T.D - IT25103799
Primary Files BookPropertyServlet, ConfirmBookingServlet, CancelBookingServlet,
Reservation.java
Overview: Connects Buyers to Properties. Manages the lifecycle of a viewing/rental: RE-
SERVED → CONFIRMED → COMPLETED or CANCELLED.
4.4 Seller Management CRUD
Overview: Gives sellers a dedicated dashboard to view their active listings, monitor in-
quiries, and calculate generated interest/revenue.
4.5 Feedback & Review Management CRUD
Overview: Allows Buyers to submit ratings and comments on properties. Utilizes inheri-
tance to differentiate between verified and unverified reviews.
4.6 Cart and Wishlist Management CRUD
Overview: Lets buyers curate a list of preferred properties. Uses advanced data structures
to quickly search through saved favorites.
7

Module Owner R.M.R.D. Rajapaksha - IT25103734
Primary Files SellerDashboardServlet, SellerHomeServlet, SellerAnalyticsServlet
Module Owner A.M.C.M Kalhari - IT25103791
Primary Files ReviewServlet, DeleteReviewServlet, Review.java,
VerifiedReview.java
5. Special Functionalities
5.1 Custom Search Algorithms (BST + QuickSort)
Instead of relying purely on linear file scanning, the application implements custom Data
Structures:
- PropertyBST: A Binary Search Tree used in SearchSavedServlet to index a user’s fa-
vorite properties by ID. This reduces search complexity to O(log n) average time.
- QuickSort: Implemented in SortSavedServlet to dynamically sort properties by price
or title, achieving O(n log n) time complexity.
5.2 Flat-File Persistence
To demonstrate core Java I/O capabilities without external dependencies, all data is per-
sisted in .txt and .tsv files. Models use encapsulated toFileLine() methods to serial-
ize themselves into pipe-delimited strings (e.g., id|title|price|location), ensuring data
formatting logic remains tightly bound to the Model.
5.3 UI Enhancements
- Dark/Light Mode: Persisted across sessions using localStorage.
- Page Transitions: Smooth CSS fade-outs handled by vanilla JavaScript.
8

Module Owner Weerasingha B. A. S. T. P . - IT25103788
Primary Files SaveFavoriteServlet, RemoveFavoriteServlet, SearchSavedServlet

Following the ownership rules, each member took full responsibility for one CRUD module.

and property management.

inheritance logic.
6 Weerasingha B.A.S.T.P . Wishlist Saving favorites, BST searching,
QuickSort sorting.
6.2 Branching Strategy
Every member worked on a feature branch (e.g., feature/admin-crud, feature/property-
crud). The rules were: pull main before starting, do not touch files belonging to another
member , and keep commits focused.

This fundamental concept in mathematics states that if n items are put into m containers, with n > m, then at least one container must contain more than one item.

In other words: "If you have more pigeons (items) than holes (containers), then at least one hole will be occupied by more than one pigeon."
